mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 195) triggers central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction (HOUST > 2000) and consumer spending (RSAFS > 350000) signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS > 130000) signals strong employment dynamics that boost corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 30% of periods where both UNRATE and PAYEMS meet thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 120) indicates improving manufacturing capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 120.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth (GDPC1 < 1.5% annualized) signals weakening demand conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 falls below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish monetary policy bias (FEDFUNDS < 3.5% and CPIAUCSL < 200) supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL meet thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 5% and UNRATE change > -0.2pp) signals strong labor market dynamics that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 30% of instances where UNRATE meets thresholds.
